name: Article III Standing
elements: Injury in fact;Traceability to defendant;Redressability by court action
when_applied: Threshold question in any federal action
common_distractors: Substantial federal interest;Adequate state remedy;Ripe controversy;Prudential standing

name: Injury in Fact (TransUnion)
elements: Concrete (close common-law analog);Particularized (affects plaintiff personally);Actual or imminent
common_distractors: Statutory violation alone;Generalized grievance;Procedural injury alone

name: Prudential Standing Limits
elements: No third-party rights;No generalized grievance;Within zone of interests
common_distractors: Particularized harm;Article III injury;Redressable by court

name: Flast Two-Nexus Test
elements: Nexus between status and legislative enactment;Nexus between status and constitutional limit on spending
common_distractors: General interest in lawful government;Concrete pocketbook injury;Direct appropriation

name: Ripeness (Abbott Labs)
elements: Fitness of issues for judicial decision;Hardship to parties from withholding review
common_distractors: Imminent enforcement;Standing satisfied;No alternative remedy

name: Mootness Exceptions
elements: Capable of repetition yet evading review;Voluntary cessation;Class action with named plaintiff change;Collateral consequences
common_distractors: Prudential mootness;Article III limit;Generalized injury

name: Grable Embedded FQJ
elements: Necessarily raised;Actually disputed;Substantial to federal system;No disturbance of Congressional balance
when_applied: State-law claim with embedded federal question
common_distractors: Federal preemption;Diversity present;Well-pleaded complaint

name: Well-Pleaded Complaint Rule
elements: Federal question must appear in plaintiff's properly stated claim;Cannot rest on anticipated defense;Cannot rest on plaintiff's response to defense
common_distractors: Holmes rule;Embedded question;Constitutional FQJ

name: Holmes Rule
elements: Federal law creates the cause of action;Sufficient (but not necessary) basis for FQJ
common_distractors: State law creates cause;Federal defense raised;Embedded question

name: Gibbs Supplemental Test
elements: Common nucleus of operative fact;Claims plaintiff would ordinarily expect to try together;Substantial federal question
common_distractors: Compulsory counterclaim;Required joinder;Diversity present

name: § 1367 Supplemental Jurisdiction
elements: Same case or controversy as anchor claim;Not destroying complete diversity (1367(b));Discretionary factors permit retention
common_distractors: Diversity sufficient;Federal preemption;Removal proper

name: Michigan v. Long Presumption
elements: State court decision fairly appears to rest primarily on federal law;State court does not explicitly invoke independent state grounds;Presumption: federal law controls and SC may review
common_distractors: Federal interest substantial;State interest minimal;Erie doctrine

name: Federal Common Law Test
elements: Power: federal interest justifies displacement;Discretion: choose new federal rule or borrow state
when_applied: Whether to apply federal common law
common_distractors: Erie commands state law;Article III ingredient;Congressional preemption

name: Sandoval Implied PROA
elements: Rights-creating language in text;Statutory structure indicating private enforcement;Context only clarifies text;No exclusive remedial scheme
when_applied: Modern test for implied rights of action
common_distractors: Cort four factors;Legislative history primary;Beneficiary class identified

name: Cort v. Ash Four Factors
elements: Plaintiff in class for whose benefit statute enacted;Indication of legislative intent to create remedy;Consistent with statute's purpose;Cause of action not traditionally state
common_distractors: Text and structure only;Agency interpretation controls;Comprehensive remedial scheme

name: Ziglar/Egbert Bivens Two-Step
elements: New context (different in any meaningful way);Special factors counseling hesitation;Any rational reason to defer to Congress
when_applied: Whether to extend Bivens beyond core trio
common_distractors: Cort v. Ash factors;Sandoval test;Adequate state remedy

name: Seminole Tribe Abrogation Test
elements: Congress unequivocally expressed intent to abrogate;Acted pursuant to a valid grant of constitutional authority that permits abrogation
common_distractors: Plan of convention;Officer suit fiction;14A Section 5

name: Plan of Convention Doctrine
elements: Federal power complete in itself;States consented to that power's full exercise via Constitution;State immunity would impair the federal scheme
common_distractors: Section 5 abrogation;Congressional clear statement;Common law SSI

name: Ex parte Young Officer Suit
elements: Sued state officer in personal capacity;Seeking prospective relief only;Alleging ongoing violation of federal law;No detailed remedial scheme displaces
common_distractors: Plan of convention;Section 5 abrogation;14A enforcement

name: Monell Municipal Liability
elements: Municipality is a person under § 1983;Plaintiff identifies official policy or custom;Policy/custom is moving force behind constitutional violation
common_distractors: Respondeat superior;Personal capacity;Final policymaker

name: Canton Failure-to-Train Liability
elements: Need for training was obvious;Failure reflects deliberate indifference;Failure caused the violation
common_distractors: Negligent supervision;Single-incident liability;Pattern of violations

name: Harlow Qualified Immunity
elements: Right was clearly established at time of conduct;Reasonable officer would have known conduct violated right;Particularized to factual context
common_distractors: Good faith subjective belief;Probable cause analysis;Absolute immunity

name: Blessing/Gonzaga § 1983 Statutory Rights
elements: Congress intended provision to benefit plaintiff;Right is not so vague and amorphous that enforcement strains judicial competence;Statute unambiguously imposes binding obligation
common_distractors: Implied private right of action;Comprehensive remedial scheme;Spending clause condition

name: Teague Retroactivity
elements: Identify the rule sought;Determine whether new (not dictated by prior precedent);Apply only narrow watershed and substantive exceptions on collateral review
common_distractors: AEDPA deference;Procedural default;Cause and prejudice

name: § 2254(d) AEDPA Deference
elements: State court adjudicated on the merits;Decision contrary to clearly established federal law;Or unreasonable application of clearly established federal law;Or unreasonable determination of facts
when_applied: Federal habeas review of state convictions
common_distractors: De novo review;Cause and prejudice;Procedural default

name: Procedural Default Cause and Prejudice
elements: Cause: external factor impeded compliance;Prejudice: error worked to actual and substantial disadvantage;Or actual innocence gateway
common_distractors: Independent review;AEDPA deference;Exhaustion

name: Schor Non-Article III Factors
elements: Extent of essential attributes reserved to Article III;Origin and importance of right;Concerns motivating Congress;Consent of parties
common_distractors: Public rights doctrine;Pure adjunct;Magistrate consent

name: Testa Federal Claims in State Court
elements: State courts must enforce federal law;Cannot discriminate against federal claims;Valid excuse only via neutral state rule;No nullification of federal rights
common_distractors: Anti-commandeering;Younger abstention;Removal jurisdiction
